premise: Humanity's first contact with an alien species goes well — too well. The aliens agree to every proposal, mirror every cultural gesture, and seem to have no demands of their own. A junior diplomat realizes the aliens aren't negotiating; they're studying, and the 'embassy' is a behavioral research facility.

characters.name: Ava Lindqvist
characters.description: Junior diplomatic attaché, late 20s. Swedish-Chilean. Trained in xenolinguistics but assigned to protocol because she's junior. Observant, socially awkward, notices patterns others dismiss as cultural differences. Keeps a private log of every interaction that felt 'performed.'

characters.name: Ambassador Chen Wei
characters.description: Head of the human delegation. Career diplomat, 60s. Brilliant, charismatic, desperate for first contact to be his legacy. Not stupid — he sees the same patterns Ava does — but he's decided the treaty matters more than the truth.

characters.name: Liaison Ektaa
characters.description: The aliens' primary representative. Presents as warm, curious, eager to learn. Every behavior is calibrated to produce trust. Not malicious — the research is genuinely scientific — but consent was never part of the protocol because the concept doesn't translate.
